spec_match.rationale: The page now models the WICG migrate_to, migrate_from, same-site, and allow_migration handshake and clearly labels the runtime as a simulator because manifest migration is declarative.

interactive.rationale: Scenario select, handoff select, payload range, failure toggles, reset, and run controls all change the visible migration outcome.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede and feature card now describe data preservation across the manifest migration handshake rather than a stale origin_migration field.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces missing association, cross-site rejection, opaque cache responses, large IndexedDB payloads, absent bridge code, and user-cleared data as distinct fallback states.

references.rationale: The page includes a references section with chromestatus.com/feature/5123349239955456 and the WICG origin migration spec section.

design_system.rationale: The touched CSS uses design-system variables, controls have explicit text/background pairs, and the concept remains an interactive demo rather than a static code card.

summary: Major findings resolved: the concept is spec-aligned, has a ChromeStatus reference, and includes live controls for success and failure states.
